summary: Completed testing of Liminal Group LLC Next.js marketing website. Found major scroll reveal animation issue preventing content visibility, but verified HTML structure and core functionality are working correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Main content not visible on page load due to reveal animations not triggering properly
ui_bugs.details: Elements with .reveal class have opacity:0 and translateY(28px), but IntersectionObserver doesn't add .visible class

ui_bugs.issue: Next.js hydration mismatch causing style inconsistencies
ui_bugs.details: Server-client style mismatch on form inputs with caret-color: transparent

integration_issues.issue: Cannot fully test smooth scrolling due to content visibility issues
Cannot test mobile hamburger menu due to content visibility issues
Cannot fully test audit form due to visibility issues

passed_tests: Next.js app builds and starts successfully
All HTML sections render correctly in DOM
Header sticky navigation structure is present
All navigation links have correct data-testid attributes
Form elements are properly structured with validation attributes
Mobile navigation overlay HTML structure is correct
All required sections (Hero, Services, Process, Demos, Audit, About, CTA, Footer) are present
Page metadata and SEO elements are correctly configured
success_percentage: Frontend: 30% - Core structure working but major visibility issues prevent full testing
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix scroll reveal animation system. The IntersectionObserver in useEffect is not properly adding 'visible' class to elements with 'reveal' class, causing all main content to be hidden (opacity: 0). This prevents testing of navigation scrolling, mobile menu, form validation, and other interactive features. Recommend either fixing the IntersectionObserver logic or temporarily removing reveal animations for testing.
